note: ET translator added byline absent from EN — see design D3 case 5

sourceText: Ominous warning—which I failed to heed.
note: One-line aside "Ominous warning—which I failed to heed." merged into ET p005 alongside EN p006
translation: Kurjakuulutav hoiatus – mida ma ei võtnud kuulda.

note: Bridging phrase "But let him tell you:" merged into ET p002 tail
Editorial aside "(Bill D. now continues his story.)" merged into ET q033 tail — spec D3 case 6 option (b)

sourceText: The last few months were filled with fear and self-pity. I began to contemplate suicide with increasing regularity, yet I was afraid of dying. I remember thinking that this life would go on and on, never getting better and slowly fading away to nothing.
note: Short fear/suicide-contemplation paragraph merged into ET p015 tail
translation: Viimased kuud olid täis hirmu ja enesehaletsust. Hakkasin üha sagedamini mõtlema enesetapule, ja ometi kartsin surra. Mäletan, kuidas mõtlesin, et see elu kestab ikka ja edasi, ei lähe kunagi paremaks ja hääbub aeglaselt olematusse.

sourceText: In Alcoholics Anonymous, I knew I had found a protective haven. But during the ensuing 4 1⁄2 years I fell into the category known, in A.A. parlance, as a “chronic slipper.” I might get a good six months of sobriety under my belt, but then I would get a bottle to celebrate.
note: Content folded into tail of ET p012 (2:1 ET merge at "In Alcoholics Anonymous, I knew I had found a protective haven")
translation: Anonüümsetes Alkohoolikutes teadsin, et olin leidnud kaitsva pelgupaiga. Kuid järgnenud 4½ aasta jooksul kuulusin kategooriasse, keda AA kõnepruugis nimetatakse „krooniliseks libastujaks". Mul võis olla kuue kuu pikkune kainus käes, ent siis võtsin pudeli, et seda tähistada.

note: LISAD section header; no EN equivalent

sourceText: In 1951 the Lasker Award was given Alcoholics Anonymous. The citation reads in part as follows:
note: Lasker citation intro — ET edition does not contain this citation (ET page 603 in source PDF has only "IV LASKERI PREEMIA" + "Neile," fragment then jumps to Appendix V). Not an extraction defect; ET source text is incomplete.
translation: 1951. aastal anti Anonüümsetele Alkohoolikutele Laskeri preemia. Põhjendus kõlab osaliselt nõnda:

sourceText: “The American Public Health Association presents a Lasker Group Award for 1951 to Alcoholics Anonymous in recognition of its unique and highly successful approach to that age-old public health and social problem, alcoholism . . . In emphasizing alcoholism as an illness, the social stigma associated with this condition is being blotted out . . . Historians may one day recognize Alcoholics Anonymous to have been a great venture in social pioneering which forged a new instrument for social action; a new therapy based on the kinship of common suffering; one having a vast potential for the myriad other ills of mankind.”
note: Lasker citation body — ET edition does not contain this citation (same source gap as p002).
translation: „Ameerika Rahvatervise Assotsiatsioon annab Anonüümsetele Alkohoolikutele 1951. aasta Laskeri grupipreemia tunnustusena tema ainulaadse ja äärmiselt eduka lähenemise eest sellele ammusele rahvatervise- ja ühiskondlikule probleemile – alkoholismile . . . Rõhutades alkoholismi kui haigust, kustutatakse selle seisundiga seotud sotsiaalne häbimärk . . . Ajaloolased võivad ühel päeval tunnistada, et Anonüümsed Alkohoolikud oli suurepärane sotsiaalse pioneeritöö ettevõtmine, mis sepitses uue tööriista ühiskondlikuks tegevuseks; uue teraapia, mis põhineb ühise kannatuse sugulusel; teraapia, millel on tohutu potentsiaal inimkonna lugematute teiste hädade jaoks."

sourceText: Need „lühikesel kujul“ Konseptsioonid võeti vastu 1971. aastal Teenistuse Üldkonverentsil.
note: ET-specific standalone sentence on 1971 adoption; EN folds equivalent content into preceding paragraph
translation: These "short form" Concepts were adopted in 1971 at the General Service Conference.
